core_payload.content:
# Tracker Planning Feedback

## 1. Role
Tracker Planning Feedback owns the loop where execution truth changes future intent. It feeds drift, overload, and pacing evidence back into planning and operator explanation so the system can re-sequence work before commitments fully fail.

## 2. Planning Feedback Surface
This sub-hub groups `planner`, `planning-horizon-engine`, `personal-ai-assistant`, and `ai-control-surface`. Together they define how Tracker evidence becomes replanning, horizon compression, operator explanation, and visible control over the next corrective move.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own raw work objects, notification timing, or runtime queue execution. Those responsibilities belong to `tracked-objects`, `schedule-pressure`, and `orchestration-signals`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Tracker Planning Feedback groups the replanning loop of the tracker system: Planner, Planning Horizon Engine, Personal AI Assistant, and AI Control Surface. It keeps overload evidence, drift explanation, horizon compression, and operator-facing corrective choices together so execution truth can shape the next plan before commitments fully fail. This boundary matters because tracking becomes useful only when evidence can alter intent in a legible and timely way. It creates one explicit place where telemetry becomes revised sequencing, explanation, and corrective operator choice.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Dream Tracker decomposition to isolate replanning, horizon compression, and operator explanation from timing pressure and downstream orchestration feeds.
